medical_history.chief_complaint: Profound hearing loss in both ears
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Noticed hearing difficulty at 6 months old, started using hearing aids at 9 months old, no further benefit from hearing aids after 3 years of use
medical_history.past_medical_history: No systemic anomalies, normal neurological examination and normal intellectual development

physical_examination.specialty_examination: HRCT scan showed no signal on the left cochlear region and narrow internal auditory canal on the left side, and cochlear malformation like in Mondini deformity and dilated vestibule on the right side. MRI scan showed absent cochlea and cochleovestibular nerve on the left side.

laboratory_examinations.findings: Severe hearing loss in both ears

radiographic_examinations.findings: No signal on the left cochlear region and narrow internal auditory canal on the left side. Wide basal turn with no separation between middle and apical turn like in Mondini deformity, vestibular anomalies such as large vestibular aquaduct and dilated vestibule on the right side
Failed to show cochlea and cochleovestibular nerve on the left side. Malformed cochlea with enlarged endolymphatic sac on the right side
Electrode array was in the cochlea, no evidence of insertion of the electrode array into the internal auditory canal

other_tests.changes_over_time: Improved to 25/40 on the third month and 31/40 on the sixth month postoperatively

diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Cochlear and cochleovestibular nerve aplasia on the left side and incomplete partition on the right side
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was based on HRCT and MRI scans showing no signal on the left cochlear region and narrow internal auditory canal on the left side, and a wide basal turn with no separation between middle and apical turns on the right side, resembling Mondini deformity. Vestibular anomalies such as large vestibular aqueduct and dilated vestibule were also observed on the right side.
